title: North Devon: Strand river wall repairs at Barnstaple Long Bridge (tendered works)
description: North Devon District Council published a tender for repairs to the Strand river wall adjacent to Long Bridge in Barnstaple, including dismantling and rebuilding a downstream section and completing additional repair works, supporting flood resilience and the integrity of a key riverside asset.
statusRationale: A Find a Tender notice has been published describing the river wall repair scope and procurement timeline, indicating the works are moving into delivery.
locationDescription: Strand river wall adjacent to Barnstaple Long Bridge (River Taw), Barnstaple, North Devon, England
locationSource: Find a Tender notice NDC0056-25 (North Devon District Council) for Strand River Wall Repair Works, specified as 'Strand river wall adjacent to Long Bridge, Barnstaple'

evidence.title: NDC0056-25 Strand River Wall Repair Works (tender notice)
evidence.source: Find a Tender (North Devon District Council)
evidence.summary: Tender notice for reactive maintenance works to the Strand river wall adjacent to Long Bridge, Barnstaple, including rebuild and repair sections.

title: Stratford-on-Avon: Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne rail link SOBC refresh (Restoring Your Railway)
description: Stratford-on-Avon District Council progressed work on a refreshed Strategic Outline Business Case (SOBC) for reinstating the rail link between Stratford-upon-Avon and Honeybourne, positioning the proposal as evidence for strategic planning and potential future funding bids.
statusRationale: The council has approved and published decision records for the SOBC refresh, but reinstatement remains contingent on further development, funding and external approvals.
locationDescription: Representative midpoint for the former Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne rail corridor, approximated at Stratford-upon-Avon (Warwickshire) as the named project origin and key node on the reinstatement proposal.
locationSource: Project title and council records referencing the Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne rail link (Stratford-on-Avon District Council decision details and press release).

evidence.title: Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne Railway Line - Strategic Outline Business Case Study Refresh (decision details)
evidence.summary: Cabinet decision record noting the refreshed SOBC for the Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne rail link and its intended use as evidence for strategic planning.

evidence.title: Stratford-upon-Avon to Honeybourne Railway Line - Strategic Outline Business Case refresh to be submitted to DfT
evidence.summary: Council press release describing the refreshed SOBC and next steps, including submission to the Department for Transport.

title: Wiltshire: A303 Stonehenge scheme cancelled and Development Consent Order revoked
description: The Department for Transport confirmed the A303 Amesbury to Berwick Down (Stonehenge) scheme would not proceed and the existing Development Consent Order (DCO) would be revoked, ending the nationally significant road/tunnel proposal in Wiltshire.
statusRationale: The Department for Transport stated the scheme will not proceed and published a revocation order for the DCO, indicating the project has been cancelled.
locationDescription: Stonehenge World Heritage Site area on the A303 corridor between Amesbury and Berwick Down, Wiltshire (centred on Stonehenge).
locationSource: Project description references the A303 Amesbury to Berwick Down (Stonehenge) scheme; representative centroid set to Stonehenge coordinates.

evidence.title: Statement on the A303 Stonehenge scheme
evidence.summary: DfT statement confirming the A303 Stonehenge scheme will not proceed and the Development Consent Order will be revoked.

evidence.title: Notice of Revocation Order (A303 Amesbury to Berwick Down)
evidence.summary: Revocation order notice document for the A303 Amesbury to Berwick Down Development Consent Order.

title: Merton: ‘Remaking Morden’ regeneration funding approved and partner procurement progressed
description: Merton Council announced and then advanced formal decision-making for an initial £30 million investment to progress the ‘Remaking Morden’ regeneration programme, enabling masterplanning and the appointment of a development partner for town-centre renewal.
statusRationale: The council has committed capital funding and taken cabinet decisions to progress the programme and procure a partner, but major physical delivery remains subject to future approvals and phased implementation.
locationDescription: Morden town centre (around Morden Underground Station), London Borough of Merton, London
locationSource: Project description and Merton Council Newsroom/cabinet decision records referring to the ‘Remaking Morden’ town-centre regeneration programme

evidence.title: Merton Council unveils exciting plans for Morden with £30m investment
evidence.summary: Council announcement setting out the £30m investment to accelerate Remaking Morden and outlining intended regeneration outcomes.

evidence.title: Remaking Morden Programme (cabinet decision details)
evidence.summary: Cabinet decision record relating to the Remaking Morden Programme, including steps to progress delivery and procurement.

title: Milton Keynes: Wolverton Agora regeneration and public-realm works (delayed, start targeted for 2026)
description: Milton Keynes City Council continued progressing the Wolverton Agora regeneration, including the Old Market Hall and surrounding public realm, with updated timelines indicating delays but an intention to start main works from 2026 as part of a wider Wolverton town-centre renewal programme.
statusRationale: Council and local reporting indicate the scheme remains active but has been delayed, with start/delivery dates re-phased rather than completed.
locationDescription: Wolverton Agora / Old Market Hall area, Wolverton town centre, Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire
locationSource: Project description and Milton Keynes City Council updates referring to the Wolverton Agora and Old Market Hall regeneration site

evidence.title: Old Market Hall set to take centre stage in Wolverton regeneration
evidence.summary: Council update on the Old Market Hall’s role within the Wolverton regeneration programme and the intended benefits for the town centre.

evidence.title: Wolverton Agora regeneration is delayed until 2028
evidence.summary: Report describing revised timelines for the Wolverton Agora regeneration programme and the updated expected opening date.

evidence.title: Wolverton Agora Site development plan agreed to move forward
evidence.summary: Local regeneration update reporting agreement to move forward with the Wolverton Agora site development plan and updated start dates.

title: Breckland: Swaffham heritage-led regeneration (Town Pound restoration and follow-on heritage funding)
description: Breckland Council progressed heritage-led regeneration in Swaffham, including completion of restoration works to the historic Town Pound and securing additional heritage funding to continue conservation and public realm improvements within the town’s conservation area.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm completion of the Town Pound restoration and subsequent funding secured for continued heritage activity, indicating delivery is under way and partially completed.
locationDescription: Swaffham town centre conservation area, Swaffham, Norfolk (including the historic Town Pound)
locationSource: Breckland Council press release: 'Restoration of Swaffham’s historic Town Pound complete' (14-03-2025) and related heritage funding update (28-08-2025)

evidence.title: Restoration of Swaffham’s historic Town Pound complete
evidence.summary: Council announcement confirming completion of restoration works to Swaffham’s historic Town Pound.

evidence.title: Breckland Council secures almost £1m heritage funding boost
evidence.summary: Council update on securing additional heritage funding to support continuation of heritage and regeneration activity in Breckland market towns, including Swaffham.

title: Doncaster: Doncaster Sheffield Airport (DSA) reopening programme – mobilisation funding and operational ramp-up
description: City of Doncaster Council continued the programme to reopen Doncaster Sheffield Airport, including cabinet-approved mobilisation funding packages and operational hiring as activity increased on site ahead of the council’s stated reopening ambitions.
statusRationale: Council statements and updates confirm continued mobilisation and staffing activity supported by funding approvals, but the airport remains in a reopening programme rather than fully operational passenger service.
locationDescription: Doncaster Sheffield Airport (formerly Robin Hood Airport Doncaster Sheffield) site, Finningley, Doncaster, South Yorkshire
locationSource: Project description and City of Doncaster Council updates referring to mobilisation and operational ramp-up at the Doncaster Sheffield Airport site

evidence.title: Airport gets financial boost to progress re-opening
evidence.source: City of Doncaster Council (Visit Doncaster news)
evidence.summary: Update reporting cabinet approval of a funding package and progress to continue mobilisation works as part of the programme to reopen the airport.

evidence.title: Doncaster Sheffield Airport statements (latest releases and programme updates)
evidence.summary: Council statements page listing airport reopening updates, including staffing and operational ramp-up milestones.

title: Hartlepool: Waterfront Connectivity Project Phase 2 active-travel and public-realm works
description: Hartlepool Borough Council began Phase 2 of the Waterfront Connectivity Project, delivering improvements to walking and cycling routes, streetlighting, signage and junction layouts around Hartlepool Marina and nearby visitor attractions to improve access and the public realm.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm works started on Phase 2, indicating the scheme is in delivery on the ground.
locationDescription: Hartlepool Marina / The Waterfront area, centred near the Victoria Terrace and Maritime Avenue junction (Hartlepool, County Durham).
locationSource: Hartlepool Borough Council project update and local reporting describing Phase 2 works around Hartlepool Marina and specifically referencing the Victoria Terrace / Maritime Avenue junction area.

evidence.title: Work begins on Waterfront Connectivity project phase two
evidence.summary: Council announcement that Phase 2 of the Waterfront Connectivity project has started, describing active-travel and public realm improvements around the waterfront.

evidence.title: Next phase of £6m project to improve connections around Hartlepool attractions
evidence.summary: Local reporting on Phase 2 works scope, including route improvements, lighting and junction changes around Hartlepool’s waterfront attractions.

title: Mansfield: Four Seasons multi-storey car park refurbishment (tendered works and delivery update)
description: Mansfield District Council progressed refurbishment works for the Four Seasons multi-storey car park in Mansfield town centre, following investigations identifying defects and the need for repairs and protection to extend the structure’s long-term durability.
statusRationale: A Find a Tender notice was published for refurbishment and the council subsequently issued project updates, indicating the refurbishment programme is progressing into delivery.
locationDescription: Four Seasons multi-storey car park, Mansfield town centre, Nottinghamshire
locationSource: Mansfield District Council update and Find a Tender notice (via Bidstats) referencing refurbishment works at the Four Seasons multi-storey car park in Mansfield town centre

evidence.title: Four Seasons multi-storey car park refurbishment (council update)
evidence.summary: Council update on the planned refurbishment/repairs programme for the Four Seasons multi-storey car park, including context and next steps.

evidence.title: Car Park Refurbishment Four Seasons Multi-Storey Car Park, Mansfield (tender notice reference)
evidence.summary: Tender notice listing for refurbishment works at the Four Seasons multi-storey car park, referencing the Find a Tender publication and scope.

title: North East Lincolnshire: Freshney Place regeneration – construction milestones and steel signing
description: North East Lincolnshire Council advanced the Freshney Place redevelopment in Grimsby, including construction start announcements and a milestone steel-signing event, repurposing parts of the centre to create a new leisure/community-focused destination as part of wider town-centre regeneration.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm construction activity has started and milestones have been reached, indicating the project is in delivery.
locationDescription: Freshney Place shopping centre, Grimsby town centre, North East Lincolnshire
locationSource: North East Lincolnshire Council project updates referencing the Freshney Place redevelopment site in Grimsby (e.g., https://www.nelincs.gov.uk/construction-starts-on-freshney-place-development-site/ and https://www.nelincs.gov.uk/freshney-place-steel-signing-sees-council-leader-put-pen-to-paper/)

evidence.title: Construction starts on Freshney Place development site
evidence.summary: Council update confirming construction has begun on the Freshney Place redevelopment in Grimsby.

evidence.title: Freshney Place steel signing sees council leader put pen to paper
evidence.summary: Council update describing a milestone steel-signing event for the Freshney Place redevelopment as construction progresses.

title: Ribble Valley: Clitheroe Castle Keep structural repairs (tendered works and council delivery start)
description: Ribble Valley Borough Council progressed a programme of structural repairs to Clitheroe Castle Keep, supporting preservation of a landmark heritage asset and visitor attraction, with procurement activity followed by a council update indicating works had started on site.
statusRationale: Tendering was published and the council later issued a public update that works had started, indicating active delivery.
locationDescription: Clitheroe Castle Keep, Castle Hill, Clitheroe, Lancashire (Ribble Valley Borough Council project site).
locationSource: Project evidence names the specific site as “Clitheroe Castle Keep” (Ribble Valley Borough Council tender listing and council update on works starting at Clitheroe Castle).

evidence.summary: Tender listing for Ribble Valley Borough Council procurement for structural repairs to Clitheroe Castle Keep.

evidence.title: Start of work on Clitheroe Castle improvement scheme
evidence.summary: Council update announcing the start of works on an improvement scheme at Clitheroe Castle.

title: Arun: Littlehampton Seafront Scheme (Levelling Up Fund) public-realm and visitor-infrastructure improvements
description: Arun District Council progressed the Levelling Up Fund-backed Littlehampton Seafront Scheme, delivering new public realm, visitor facilities and active-travel upgrades along the seafront, with council updates highlighting construction progress and final-stage works.
statusRationale: Council project updates confirm on-site progress and final-stage works on the seafront scheme, indicating delivery is under way.
locationDescription: Littlehampton seafront, centred on East Beach / South Terrace area (near Littlehampton Harbour mouth), Littlehampton, Arun District, West Sussex
locationSource: Arun District Council updates for the Littlehampton Seafront Scheme describing works along Littlehampton seafront including East Beach / South Terrace / Windmill Road areas

title: Braintree: Witham Community Centre construction milestones (groundbreaking and topping out)
description: Braintree District Council advanced delivery of the new Witham Community Centre, with public milestones including a groundbreaking ceremony and a topping out event, supporting new civic/community estate provision in Witham.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm construction activity and milestones, indicating the project is in delivery rather than a concept stage.
locationDescription: Witham, Essex (representative town-centre point for the new Witham Community Centre project in Braintree District)
locationSource: Braintree District Council project updates referenced in evidence (groundbreaking and topping out announcements for the new Witham Community Centre); specific site address not provided so town-centre centroid used

evidence.title: Witham Community Centre - groundbreaking ceremony marks start of construction
evidence.summary: Council announcement marking the start of construction of the new Witham Community Centre.

evidence.title: Witham Community Centre celebrates topping out milestone

title: Gedling: Greater Carlton regeneration programme governance for £20m neighbourhood investment
description: Gedling Borough Council progressed governance and programme development for a Greater Carlton neighbourhood regeneration programme associated with £20 million of government investment, establishing/strengthening local board arrangements to oversee delivery of a long-term regeneration plan for Carlton and surrounding areas.
statusRationale: Council updates confirm programme governance is being established and plans are being developed, but the programme is primarily at planning/enablement stage rather than fully built-out delivery.
locationDescription: Carlton town centre, Carlton (Greater Carlton area including Carlton Hill), Gedling Borough, Nottinghamshire
locationSource: Project described as covering the Greater Carlton area (Carlton, Carlton Hill and surrounding neighbourhoods) in Gedling Borough Council materials; representative point placed at Carlton town centre.

evidence.title: New £20m Carlton plan unveiled (Regeneration Plan for Greater Carlton)
evidence.summary: Council press release describing the unveiling of a regeneration plan and programme intent for Greater Carlton linked to £20m investment.

evidence.title: New Chair appointed to lead £20 million Carlton investment plan
evidence.summary: Council update announcing the appointment of an independent chair to the Greater Carlton Neighbourhood Board overseeing delivery of the £20m investment plan.

title: Mole Valley: Transform Leatherhead – major planning application submitted for Swan Centre and Bull Hill redevelopment
description: A major planning application was submitted to Mole Valley District Council for the redevelopment of the Swan Centre and Bull Hill area in Leatherhead under the Transform Leatherhead programme, proposing new homes, reconfigured retail/leisure, public space and updated town-centre infrastructure.
statusRationale: Reports confirm a planning application has been lodged and is under consideration; delivery depends on planning approval and subsequent procurement and funding arrangements.
locationDescription: Swan Centre and Bull Hill area, Leatherhead town centre, Surrey (site centroid approximation)
locationSource: Project description and reporting on the Transform Leatherhead planning submission for Swan Centre and Bull Hill redevelopment (Build News; Surrey Chambers of Commerce)

evidence.title: Leatherhead regeneration plans lodged for 480 homes and Swan Centre overhaul
evidence.summary: Construction-industry reporting that a major planning application has been submitted for the Swan Centre and Bull Hill redevelopment in Leatherhead as part of Transform Leatherhead.

evidence.title: Plans submitted for major redevelopment of Leatherhead town centre
evidence.summary: Business community update summarising the planning submission for Transform Leatherhead, including proposed housing and town-centre improvements.

title: Warrington: Liverpool Road (Sankey Bridges) flood risk management scheme (pump station and drainage upgrades)
description: Warrington Borough Council progressed a flood risk management scheme at Liverpool Road (Sankey Bridges), including drainage upgrades and a new pump station intended to reduce flooding disruption on a key transport corridor and improve local resilience.
statusRationale: Council announcements confirm the scheme moved into on-site works and subsequent updates report continuing construction, indicating active delivery.
locationDescription: Sankey Bridges area on Liverpool Road (A57), Warrington, Cheshire (representative point for the Liverpool Road/Sankey Bridges flood risk management works corridor).
locationSource: Project described as located at Liverpool Road (Sankey Bridges), Warrington (Warrington Borough Council news release and local reporting).

evidence.title: Liverpool Road flood scheme to reduce disruption and help prevent flooding
evidence.summary: Council announcement describing the Liverpool Road flood risk management scheme scope including drainage upgrades and a pump station.

evidence.title: Liverpool Road flood scheme: update as construction continues
evidence.summary: Local news update reporting continued construction activity and ongoing works on the Liverpool Road flood scheme.

title: Richmond upon Thames: Twickenham Riverside regeneration – construction start and delivery milestones
description: Richmond Council progressed the Twickenham Riverside regeneration, advancing construction arrangements for a new riverside park and town-centre improvements alongside housing and ground-floor uses, with council updates confirming key delivery milestones.
statusRationale: Council press releases confirm contract and construction milestones for the scheme, indicating active delivery rather than a concept stage.
locationDescription: Twickenham Riverside regeneration site (around Water Lane / Wharf Lane by the River Thames, near Twickenham town centre), Twickenham, London Borough of Richmond upon Thames
locationSource: Project description citing “Twickenham Riverside (Water Lane / Wharf Lane / King Street links)” and Richmond upon Thames Council updates on the Twickenham Riverside regeneration scheme

evidence.title: Twickenham Riverside: main contractor appointed ahead of construction

evidence.title: Construction begins on Twickenham Riverside regeneration scheme

title: Southwark: civic square works at Peckham Rye Station (public realm, safety and market space)
description: Southwark Council began works to deliver a new civic square at Peckham Rye Station, including public realm upgrades, seating, lighting, CCTV and new space for markets and events, improving a key town-centre gateway next to the rail station.
statusRationale: Southwark Council announced the start of works and Network Rail confirmed associated funding for station-area improvements, indicating delivery is under way.
locationDescription: Peckham Rye Station forecourt / station square (civic square works), Peckham, London Borough of Southwark
locationSource: Project description and cited Southwark Council announcement referencing works at Peckham Rye Station civic square/forecourt

evidence.title: Work begins on new civic square at Peckham Rye Station
evidence.summary: Council announcement confirming the start of works to create a new civic square at Peckham Rye Station, including public realm upgrades and improved facilities.

evidence.summary: Network Rail media release announcing funding for Peckham Rye Station improvement elements and describing next steps and scope.
